schema.appDescription: Calculate monthly EPF and EPS contribution split between employee and employer. Understand where your 12% EPF goes — EPF vs EPS.

inputs.label: Voluntary PF (% extra)
inputs.hint: VPF is on top of mandatory 12%

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the EPF Contribution Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is the difference between EPF and EPS?
faq.a: EPF (Employee Provident Fund): both employee 12% and employer 3.67% go here — you get this with interest on retirement/resignation. EPS (Employee Pension Scheme): employer's 8.33% (capped at ₹1,250/month) goes to EPS — this funds a monthly pension after age 58 with 10+ years of service. You don't receive EPS corpus directly.

faq.q: What is VPF and should I invest in it?
faq.a: VPF (Voluntary Provident Fund) is additional contribution beyond mandatory 12%. It earns the same EPF rate (8.25%) and has the same EEE tax treatment. For risk-free, tax-free guaranteed returns, VPF is superior to most fixed income alternatives. However, it is illiquid until retirement.

EPF interest is tax-free for employee contributions up to ₹2.5 lakh/year (₹5 lakh for government employees). Interest on contributions above this threshold is taxable. Employer contributions up to 12% of basic are tax-free. Withdrawal after 5 years of continuous service is tax-free.

faq.q: Is the EPF Contribution Breakup Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, completely free on CalcPhi with no registration.
No. All calculations run in your browser. Your data is never stored.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: ₹30,000 basic — EPF/EPS split
examples.text: Monthly contributions: Employee PF = ₹3,600 (12%). Employer: EPF = ₹1,101 (3.67%), EPS = ₹1,250 (capped). Total to EPF account = ₹4,701. Total monthly EPF-related deduction = ₹7,201 from combined CTC. EPS ₹1,250 is separate — not in your EPF account.

examples.heading: High earner — above ₹15,000 basic
examples.text: For ₹1 lakh basic: Employee PF = ₹12,000. Employer EPS = ₹1,250 (capped). Employer EPF = ₹12,000 × 3.67% = ₹4,404. Total EPF account credit = ₹16,404/month. Higher earners benefit most from EPF as tax-free compounding grows significantly.
